message:
fix(h2): preserve pending_ops ordering in next_frame via skipped/newly_queued split

Replaces the in-place push_front/push_back mutations during the
next_frame iteration loop with a structured three-deque approach:
1. newly_queued_ops: CONTINUATION frames that must be emitted next
   (RFC 9113 §6.10 requires no interleaving after HEADERS)
2. skipped_ops: DATA frames that could not send due to flow control
3. remaining self.pending_ops: untouched tail of the original queue

After the loop, the deques are prepended in priority order
(newly_queued first, then skipped, then remainder), ensuring that:
- CONTINUATION always immediately follows its HEADERS
- Flow-control-blocked DATA maintains relative ordering
- Other pending ops that were never visited stay at the back

This eliminates subtle ordering violations where push_front of a
remainder chunk could leapfrog over a CONTINUATION that was also
push_fronted in the same iteration.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
